title: Message Envelopes, Bytes, and Schema Evolution · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs message envelopes, bytes, and schema evolution from producer, broker, queue, delivery, Go runtime, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one protocol or concurrency misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a bounded Go envelope decoder and compatibility matrix after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Define message ID, type, schema version, source, subject, occurrence time, correlation, causation, tenant, and trace context without conflating them; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Validate unknown envelopes and payloads after bounded decoding with required fields, semantic checks, collection limits, and explicit rejection evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Buffer Decoding Boundary · changed case · Review Buffer Decoding Boundary
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go messaging team handles case 8297 during outbox relay recovery after a database commit. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound bytes and validate content metadata, identity, and version before decoding effects; keep message identity out of metric labels while preserving causal traces; then exhaust retry age before attempts reach their maximum. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Which action demonstrates Buffer Decoding Boundary without dropping earlier contracts?

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use AsyncAPI 3.1 and CloudEvents 1.0.2 selectively to document channels, operations, messages, bindings, examples, and event metadata while testing runtime truth; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review Go messaging team handles case 4253 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound bytes and validate content metadata, identity, and version before decoding effects; prove cleanup under a single deadline and cancellation cause; then cancel the parent context while a result is still ambiguous. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use AsyncAPI 3.1 and CloudEvents 1.0.2 selectively to document channels, operations, messages, bindings, examples, and event metadata while testing runtime truth; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A valid AsyncAPI document proves deployed routing and message behavior conform. In Go, decoding JSON into a struct is not complete runtime validation.
